competition.name: Stella Zhang New Venture Competition
competition.host_school: UCI Paul Merage School of Business

naming_gift.initial_gift_year_note: Initial gift announced January 2022 per Merage press release; the underlying term begins as early as the 2021 NVC.
naming_gift.naming_through_year_note: Computed as 2026 + 5 (extension term). The Merage March 2025 release describes 'a $750,000 commitment to extend her annual gift over the next five years' without naming an explicit end year.
naming_gift.bridge_to_competition: Zarina Bahadur (Baby Box, MIE '20, 2020 Grand Prize) joined Zhang's Long Beach Accelerator portfolio, exposing Zhang to the NVC.

competition.sponsors_note: Sponsors fund the program collectively. Per-track corporate sponsorship does not exist in this competition's structure; tracks are funded from the pool and named only by category.

mentor_structure.note: Coach and judge roles overlap; named individuals like Dan Jenkins, David Ochi, and Larry Wilk function as both.

sample_workshops: How to Come up with a Billion Dollar Idea
Is there a Market for Your Product?

grand_finale.venue: SB1 Merage Auditorium, 4291 Pereira Drive, Suite 2300, Irvine, CA 92627

years.prize_pool_announced: Over $100,000 plus AWS credits for all semi-finalists
years.finalists_note: 10 finalists per Merage NVC page as of 2026-05-12: two per track. An earlier archive pass recorded 'AegleBio, Billify, NeuroBoost, Grad2Go' as named semi-finalists; those teams do not appear on the current finalist page and could not be re-verified against a primary source on 2026-05-12.

years.theme: Sustainability, Student Life Solutions, and Medical Tech
years.prize_pool_note: Pool jumped from $100,000+ to $150,000+ following Stella Zhang's $750,000 March 2025 naming-gift extension; see competition.naming_gift.
confirmed_judges_partial: Richard Matthew (Blum Center Faculty Director, Social Enterprise track judge)
years.judges_note: Full grand-finale judging panel not preserved in indexed press materials; likely published in the password-protected 2025 NVC report PDF.

awards.project: A sustainable furniture resale and storage service for students during move-out/move-in cycles to reduce waste.
Smart dental retainer case with UV-C sanitization and Bluetooth tracking capabilities.
AI-powered wellness companion (the AI assistant is named 'Chloe') for women with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), with tracking, community, and support features.
members: Ricianne "Chi" Rey (MIE; co-founder and Marketing Lead per RocketReach 2026-05-12)
Varshini Vijay (tech team; UCI student, Zotbotics Robotics Club co-president per LinkedIn)
Benson Manzano (tech team; UCI ICS student, FUSION at UCI ICS project director per LinkedIn)
awards.members_note: Roster expanded 2026-05-12 from the prior single-name listing. Ricianne Rey directly verified via antrepreneur.uci.edu/meet-chi-rey/ profile; the additional four members were surfaced via WebSearch aggregation cross-referenced with Varshini Vijay's and Benson Manzano's UCI-affiliated LinkedIn profiles. A fifth teammate (first name 'Kimberly') is mentioned in search-engine summaries without a confirmed last name and is not added here. Per Ricianne Rey's antrepreneur.uci.edu profile, the team competed as MIE-program peers.

awards.project: Passive thermal/optical vital-sign monitoring system for seniors ("Birdhouse by Oslo").

Reverse-bidding auction platform connecting homeowners with home service providers.

Biodegradable leather handbags made from waste coconut water.
AI-powered wellness companion (the AI assistant is named 'Chloe') for women with Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), with tracking, community, and support features.

Wireless, child-friendly handheld nasometry device for speech therapy in cleft-palate patients. Quantitatively evaluates hypernasality and other resonance disorders; replaces bulky, stationary, intimidating-to-children clinic-based nasometers.
members: Dave Soliven (UCI BME 4th-year undergrad, Management minor)

awards.project: Passive thermal/optical vital-sign monitoring system for seniors ("Birdhouse by Oslo").
AI-powered app for real-time analysis of water quality test strips and samples.
A sustainable furniture resale and storage service for students during move-out/move-in cycles to reduce waste.

awards.project: AI-enhanced dashcam system that detects trucker drowsiness without violating privacy (no recording when stopped).
awards.members_note: Member spellings preserved from the primary Business Wire / Merage 2024 press release. The UCI Beall and Butterworth 2024 winners page (bbcomp.tech.uci.edu/past/past-2024) and the ANTrepreneur Center 2024-09 post-competition profile both use 'Nathan Ampudia' (vs 'Natan Ampudia' in Business Wire) and 'Spike O'Carroll' (vs 'Spike O'Carrol' in Business Wire) - this cross-source inconsistency is noted, with the more authoritative Merage / Business Wire press release retained here. The ANTrepreneur 2024-09 profile also names a Redford Hudson (Data Science) as part of the post-competition team; not added to the original 6-member NVC roster pending confirmation whether he was on the competing team or joined afterward.
also_won: 1st Place, Butterworth Product Development Competition 2024 ($10,000; sibling UCI competition)
2nd Place, C&C Innovative Solutions SoCal Super Pitch Competition 2024 ($1,250; per ANTrepreneur Center 2024-09 profile)

awards.project: Customizable, 3D-printed ear molds for non-surgical correction of newborn ear deformities.

The UCI 2023 NVC Help Belt team is a distinct, separately-conceived seat-belt mobility-aid project. Roster for the UCI team remains Not Listed.

pre_2017_editions.disclosed_cash_total_note: $15,000 (LAS) + $14,000 (ValveStrong: $7.5K + $6K + $500) + $33,500 (NoreGen: $3.5K + $30K SoM Life Sciences Award) + $2,500 (Nobles) + $2,500 (Bloom Diagnostics). Excludes $2,750 in-kind TechPortal Calit2 Residency Award to MEGAL!NE. Roster and prize details verified against Samueli engineering primary source 2026-05-12.

pre_2017_editions.disclosed_cash_total_note: EmbryLux $20K (dual award) + Dental Insights $10K + FunBand $10K + Negocios & Etc. $10K + Sher Biomedical $10K + Carbo $5K + Jam $5K + CeleriBio $5K + Curaflow $5K + Bottle Rocket $1K + Maslow $1.5K = $82,500. UCI News reported 'more than $100,000 in cash and prizes' which appears to include legal services and workspace access not captured here. Track placements verified against UCI News primary source 2026-05-12 (previously unknown per BACKLOG Priority 4).

awards: Blum Center for Poverty Alleviation Award

disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2021_note: Updated 2026-05-12: corrected from $46,000 to $41,000. MoodCloud and REMSYS tied for Consumer Services 1st and split a single $5,000 prize ($2,500 each), not $5,000 each as previously recorded. Prior context: Shaka $17,500 (GP + BP 1st combined per Merage source) added on 2026-05-11 to the previous $28,500 disclosed; this pass drops the MoodCloud/REMSYS double-count of $5,000.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2018_note: Updated 2026-05-12: added Tech Surge 3rd ($5,000 LaserFocus) + 4th ($2,500 EchoSol) per Merage 2018 primary source. Prior total $92,500 missed these line items.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2017_note: Updated 2026-05-12 from $64,500: added Tech Surge honorable mentions Syntr Health Technologies ($2,500) + NanoShield Biotix ($2,500), Salux Diagnostics Outstanding Undergraduate Team award ($1,000), and the co-located Designing Solutions for Poverty Alleviation Blum Center runners-up Loy Loy ($5,000) + Pure Game ($5,000), and reclassifies Esqalate's $10K under that same co-located Blum Center competition.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2016_note: Added 2026-05-12: full track placements and prize amounts recovered from UCI News primary source. EmbryLux $20K dual-award + Dental Insights $10K + FunBand $10K + Negocios $10K + Sher Biomedical $10K + Carbo / Jam / CeleriBio / Curaflow $5K each + Bottle Rocket $1K + Maslow $1.5K.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2013_note: Added 2026-05-12: full sub-winner rosters and prize details recovered from Samueli engineering primary source. LAS $15K + ValveStrong $14K ($7.5K + $6K Orange Residency + $500 Concept Paper) + NoreGen $33.5K ($3.5K + $30K SoM Life Sciences Award) + Nobles $2.5K + Bloom Diagnostics $2.5K. Excludes $2,750 in-kind TechPortal Calit2 award to MEGAL!NE.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.total_2017_to_2025_note: Updated 2026-05-12 to $821,000: previous $772,500 + 2017 +$21,000 (Tech Surge HMs, Outstanding Undergrad, Loy Loy/Pure Game Blum Center co-located) + 2018 +$7,500 (Tech Surge 3rd/4th). 2017 column now reflects the full slate listed in the Merage 2017 press release.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.total_pre_2017_note: Pre-2017 cash totals are confirmed only for 2013 ($67,500) and 2016 ($82,500). 2009, 2012, 2014 partial totals are known but team-by-team rosters incomplete; 2010, 2011, 2015 remain structural gaps in the indexed public record.
